name: Metabase
description: Open source business intelligence and analytics platform. Create dashboards and explore data without writing SQL.
summary: Metabase is actively releasing updates across multiple supported version branches simultaneously. The latest release is v0.60.2 (Open Source) / v1.60.2 (Enterprise), published on April 22, 2025. Multiple stable branches (v54, v55, v57, v58, v59, v60) are receiving concurrent patch updates, indicating a mature multi-version support policy. Full changelogs are linked per release at https://github.com/metabase/metabase/releases.

answers.What is the current license?: Open Source edition uses AGPL v3; Enterprise edition uses a commercial license
answers.Is the project actively maintained?: Yes — multiple patch releases across several version branches as recently as April 22, 2025
answers.Is there a cloud-hosted version availabl: Yes — Metabase Cloud is available at metabase.com
answers.Does it support SSO and SAML authenticat: Yes — SSO and SAML are supported in the Enterprise edition
answers.What databases/data sources are supporte: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, SQL Server, BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift, Athena, SQLite, MongoDB, Druid, Oracle, Presto/Trino, Spark SQL, and more (connector details not listed in this changelog excerpt)

alternatives.name: Redash
alternatives.review: Open source, query-focused, simpler feature set.

alternatives.name: Apache Superset
alternatives.review: Apache-licensed, rich visualisation library, steeper setup curve.

alternatives.name: Grafana
alternatives.review: Primarily metrics/time-series but increasingly used for general BI.

alternatives.name: Hex
alternatives.review: Close source, enterprise focus, AI Analytics Platform for your whole team

alternatives.name: Tableau
alternatives.review: Tableau offers best-in-class visualizations and enterprise features but is closed-source and significantly more expensive than Metabase.

alternatives.name: Lightdash
alternatives.review: Lightdash integrates tightly with dbt for a metrics-layer-first approach, making it more structured than Metabase but requiring dbt adoption as a prerequisite.
